title: Massive LMS log data analysis for the early prediction of course agnostic student performance

multi_select.name: Backtested with data from Previous Students

title.plain_text: Massive LMS log data analysis for the early prediction of course agnostic student performance

tables.heading: Summary Table PRED
rows: Being at Risk of Failing in a Course - Grade in Course Calculated by Authors
15944 students from the University of Oviedo

[UrlViewTime1, AccomplishMandatory, QuizViewTime1, CourseViewtime1, UrlViewTime2, AccomplishMandatoryGrade, QuizViewTime2, CourseViewtime2, UrlViewTime3, AccomplishMandatoryPctGraded, QuizViewTime3, CourseViewtime3, UrlViewTime4, AccomplishMandatoryPercentileGrade, QuizViewTimePct, CourseViewtime4, UrlViewTime5, AccomplishOptional, QuizAttemptTime1, CourseViewtime5, UrlViewTimePct, AccomplishOptional, QuizAttemptTime1, CourseViewtime5, AssignViewTime1, AccomplishOptionalGrade, QuizAttemptTime2, CourseViewtimePct, AssignViewTime2, AccomplishOptionalPctGraded, QuizAttemptTime3, ResourceViewTime1, AssignViewTime3, AccomplishOptionalPercentileGrade, QuizAttemptTimePct, ResourceViewTime2, AssignViewTimePct, CourseViewPct, QuizCloseAttemptTime1, ResourceViewTime3, AssignSubmitTime1, ResourceViewPct, QuizCloseAttemptTime2, ResourceViewTime4, AssignSubmitTime2, ResourceViewUniquePct, QuizCloseAttemptTime3, ResourceViewTime5, AssignSubmitTime3, UrlViewPct, QuizCloseAttemptTimePct, ResourceViewTimePct, AssignSubmitTimePct, UrlViewUniquePct, AssignViewPct, AssignViewUniquePct, QuizViewPct, QuizViewUniquePct, AssignSubmitPct, AssignSubmitUniquePct, QuizAttemptPct, QuizAttemptUniquePct, QuizCloseUniquePct, QuizCloseAttemptUniquePct, ForumViewForumPct, ForumViewDiscussionPct]

All data is obtained from LMS logs. Each row represents a student-course pairing. For each pairing, the authors first decide what date would represent specific course curation thresholds and create a model for each dataset. 

The features extracted are calculated in relative terms (since different courses have different durations.

All features were normalized to range [0,1], authors also perform Feature Selection and Hyper-Parameter Tuning.

Class imbalance is addressed using multiple Resampling Techniques.
ZeroR, NB, CART, LR, MLP, SVM

Authors do a model for each course duration threshold. Selection of best algorithm is done by comparison of The AUC score.

Then, the classifier is evaluated in terms of comparison against a ZeroR and over the F1 and F0.5 scores.

All models are meant to perform early prediction and for that reason, the authors never look at the performance at 100% course duration.

Exclusive use of LMS logs. 

Limited interpretability of features due to potential differences in instructional conditions.

It is very likely that the model implementation suffers from data leakage (RFECV in a Holdout-Method).

Authors do not have access to Final Grades and compute a proxy based on Assignment marks available on Moodle for each course.
Obtaining a Passing Grade in a Course - Grade in Course Calculated by Authors
Passing Grade (1) vs Not Passing Grade (0)
15944 students from the University of Oviedo

tables.heading: Summary Table PRED
rows: 15944 students from the University of Oviedo

[UrlViewTime1, AccomplishMandatory, QuizViewTime1, CourseViewtime1, UrlViewTime2, AccomplishMandatoryGrade, QuizViewTime2, CourseViewtime2, UrlViewTime3, AccomplishMandatoryPctGraded, QuizViewTime3, CourseViewtime3, UrlViewTime4, AccomplishMandatoryPercentileGrade, QuizViewTimePct, CourseViewtime4, UrlViewTime5, AccomplishOptional, QuizAttemptTime1, CourseViewtime5, UrlViewTimePct, AccomplishOptional, QuizAttemptTime1, CourseViewtime5, AssignViewTime1, AccomplishOptionalGrade, QuizAttemptTime2, CourseViewtimePct, AssignViewTime2, AccomplishOptionalPctGraded, QuizAttemptTime3, ResourceViewTime1, AssignViewTime3, AccomplishOptionalPercentileGrade, QuizAttemptTimePct, ResourceViewTime2, AssignViewTimePct, CourseViewPct, QuizCloseAttemptTime1, ResourceViewTime3, AssignSubmitTime1, ResourceViewPct, QuizCloseAttemptTime2, ResourceViewTime4, AssignSubmitTime2, ResourceViewUniquePct, QuizCloseAttemptTime3, ResourceViewTime5, AssignSubmitTime3, UrlViewPct, QuizCloseAttemptTimePct, ResourceViewTimePct, AssignSubmitTimePct, UrlViewUniquePct, AssignViewPct, AssignViewUniquePct, QuizViewPct, QuizViewUniquePct, AssignSubmitPct, AssignSubmitUniquePct, QuizAttemptPct, QuizAttemptUniquePct, QuizCloseUniquePct, QuizCloseAttemptUniquePct, ForumViewForumPct, ForumViewDiscussionPct]

All variables were standardized and, subsequently, authors used PCA and Feature Agglomeration Techniques - deciding on FA.

Features usedd became: [URL and Assignment Access (UAA), MAndatory and Optional Assignment Evaluation (MOA), Quizz Access Time (QAT), Course and Resourve View (CIR)]
{Gap Statistic - Selection of k, ANOVA - comparison between clusters, Tukey’s Honest Significant Difference}

One clustering solution was obtained for each moment of prediction (10%, 25%, 33% and 50%). While the number of clusters remains the same, there are differences:

QAT - - : Cluster oif students who answe quizzes a lot faster.

QAT ++: Opposite of previous previous

UAA- : Students who check external resources earlier than their peers.

CIR++: Students who consistently procrastinate in course contents and resources.

MOA-CIR+: Assignment ew valuations below average while having above average views of resources.

Average: Average for all aggregate variables.

Students who answer quizzes the fastest have higher average performance while  the lowest performing student are learners who procrastinate.

The largest CIR++ (real procrastinator) achieve the second lowest average performance.
One clustering solution was obtained for each moment of prediction. The statistical tests identify at least 4 out of the 6 clusters created to have a correlation with performance.

Inferred Grade.

Lack of Additional Data sources.

Loss oif interpretability satemming from the Aggregate variables without a proper use of the remaining variables.


Clustering using a different approach would have been awesome.

blocks.text: Early detection could allow students at risk to receive timely feedback. Likewise, early identification of excellent students could allow for the delivery of more personalized content.

text.content: Early detection could allow students at risk to receive timely feedback. Likewise, early identification of excellent students could allow for the delivery of more personalized content.

rich_text.plain_text: Early detection could allow students at risk to receive timely feedback. Likewise, early identification of excellent students could allow for the delivery of more personalized content.

blocks.text: Many works either make models using data from the whole course or, in many cases, make early prediction on a single course. For those reasons, the authors use LMS logs to create a set of course agnostic models that predict student success at different thresholds of course duration: 10%, 25%, 33% and 50%.

text.content: Many works either make models using data from the whole course or, in many cases, make early prediction on a single course. For those reasons, the authors use LMS logs to create a set of course agnostic models that predict student success at different thresholds of course duration: 10%, 25%, 33% and 50%.

rich_text.plain_text: Many works either make models using data from the whole course or, in many cases, make early prediction on a single course. For those reasons, the authors use LMS logs to create a set of course agnostic models that predict student success at different thresholds of course duration: 10%, 25%, 33% and 50%.

blocks.text: Models are trained using the full logs of the Univeristy of Oviedo in the years 2013 to 2014. Different Classifiers are tested and MLP emerged as the best model throughout, achieving 80% accuracy at 10% course duration and 90% at the end of the course.

text.content: Models are trained using the full logs of the Univeristy of Oviedo in the years 2013 to 2014. Different Classifiers are tested and MLP emerged as the best model throughout, achieving 80% accuracy at 10% course duration and 90% at the end of the course.

rich_text.plain_text: Models are trained using the full logs of the Univeristy of Oviedo in the years 2013 to 2014. Different Classifiers are tested and MLP emerged as the best model throughout, achieving 80% accuracy at 10% course duration and 90% at the end of the course.

blocks.text: On a second analysis, the authors performed clustering and found 6 groups and, in 4 of the 6, there is a strong correlation between interaction patterns and student performance.

text.content: On a second analysis, the authors performed clustering and found 6 groups and, in 4 of the 6, there is a strong correlation between interaction patterns and student performance.
